claim: Combining phosphatidylinositide 3-kinase and MEK 1/2 inhibitors is ineffective at treating KRAS mutant tumors.

result.claim: Combining phosphatidylinositide 3-kinase and MEK 1/2 inhibitors is ineffective at treating KRAS mutant tumors.
result.reasoning: The claim is refuted by evidence from an animal study (Paper ID: 51889704, Credibility Rating: 2.5/5). This study explicitly states that "Combination PI3K/MEK inhibition with NVP-BKM120 and PD-0325901 induced tumor regression in a mouse model of PIK3CA wild-type, KRAS mutant colorectal cancer." This directly contradicts the claim that combining these inhibitors is ineffective. Additionally, another study (Paper ID: 33924486, Credibility Rating: 2.0/5) indicates that "Inhibitors of MEK and ERK protein kinases have shown some clinical benefits when used for the treatment of these cancers" and that "Concurrent inhibition of the RAF-MEK-ERK kinases using an intermittent treatment schedule of 3 days on-4 days off limited toxicity and efficiently inhibited tumor growth in PDX models." While this paper focuses on MEK and ERK, it supports the general principle of combination kinase inhibition being effective in RAS-driven tumors, which include KRAS mutants. The direct evidence from the animal study is strong and clearly refutes the claim.

evidence.text: Pharmacological inhibition of the IRE1α–XBP1 pathway is a therapeutic strategy for ARID1A-mutant ovarian cancers
evidence.source: Targeting the IRE1α/XBP1 Endoplasmic Reticulum Stress Response Pathway in ARID1A-Mutant Ovarian Cancers

evidence.text: BRAF and MEK kinase inhibitors are new therapeutic options for melanoma patients with activating mutations in the BRAF oncogene.
evidence.source: Melanoma and immunotherapy bridge 2015 : Naples, Italy. 1-5 December 2015.

evidence.text: New therapeutic options have been made available in recent years represented by BRAF and MEK kinase inhibitors (KIs).
evidence.source: Melanoma and immunotherapy bridge 2015 : Naples, Italy. 1-5 December 2015.

evidence.text: Concurrent inhibition of the RAF-MEK-ERK kinases using an intermittent treatment schedule of 3 days on-4 days off limited toxicity and efficiently inhibited tumor growth in PDX models.
evidence.source: Sensitivity and Resistance of Oncogenic RAS-Driven Tumors to Dual MEK and ERK Inhibition.

evidence.text: Residual disease and acquired resistance in response to EGFR inhibitors requires Aurora kinase A (AURKA) activity in epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR)-mutant lung adenocarcinoma cells.
evidence.source: Aurora kinase A drives the evolution of resistance to third-generation EGFR inhibitors in lung cancer.

evidence.text: Pan-PIM kinase inhibition did not effectively reduce viability of Burkitt lymphoma cell lines.
evidence.source: The role of histone kinases in controlling transcription in B cell lymphoma and leukaemia

evidence.text: The aim of this study was to assess the consequences of kinase inhibition for tumour cell viability in B cell lymphoma.

evidence.text: The phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase (PI3K)/protein kinase B (AKT)/mammalian target of rapamycin (mTOR) signaling pathway may be altered in luminal cancers that express the ER.
evidence.source: The potential effects of the synergistic interaction between ferulic acid and new generation CDK inhibitor anti-neoplastic drugs on breast cancer anti-tumour activity.

evidence.text: BRAF/MEK inhibitors have been shown to increase overall survival (OS) for patients with tumors harboring an activating BRAF mutation.
evidence.source: Complete metabolic response as early predictor of long-term efficacy after adoptive T cell therapy using tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes.

evidence.text: Drugs that inhibit KRAS have recently been approved for the treatment of KRAS-mutant tumors.
evidence.source: Defining the KRAS- and ERK-dependent transcriptome in KRAS-mutant cancers

evidence.text: Inhibition of HDAC6 activity using a clinically applicable small molecule inhibitor significantly improved the survival of mice bearing ARID1A-mutated tumours.
evidence.source: ARID1A-mutated ovarian cancers depend on HDAC6 activity.

evidence.text: PI3K inhibition in combination with other agents has not been studied in the context of PIK3CA wild-type, KRAS mutant cancer.
evidence.source: Combination PI3K/MEK inhibition promotes tumor apoptosis and regression in PIK3CA wild-type, KRAS mutant colorectal cancer

evidence.text: In a screen of phospho-kinases, PI3K inhibition of KRAS mutant colorectal cancer cells activated the MAPK pathway.
evidence.source: Combination PI3K/MEK inhibition promotes tumor apoptosis and regression in PIK3CA wild-type, KRAS mutant colorectal cancer
